questions.q: Subramania Bharati is celebrated chiefly as a poet writing in which language?
questions.explanation: Subramania Bharati was a Tamil poet whose patriotic verse inspired the freedom movement in the south.

questions.q: By what honorific title is Subramania Bharati popularly remembered?
questions.explanation: Bharati is widely revered as 'Mahakavi' Bharati, meaning the great poet.

questions.q: Besides poetry, Subramania Bharati was active in which profession that spread nationalist ideas?
questions.explanation: Bharati worked as a journalist and editor, using newspapers to spread patriotic and reformist ideas.

questions.q: Bharati's verse strongly championed the rights of which group within Indian society?
questions.explanation: Bharati's poetry called for the emancipation of women and condemned their oppression.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Subramania Bharati born?
questions.explanation: Bharati was born at Ettayapuram in present-day Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Subramania Bharati's songs are best described as which kind of compositions?
questions.explanation: Bharati composed devotional and fervently patriotic songs that stirred national feeling.

questions.q: Bharati supported which broad political demand of the nationalist movement?
questions.explanation: Bharati's writing openly called for India's freedom from colonial rule.

questions.q: Bharati was associated with the assertive nationalist wing inspired by which leader's call for swaraj?
questions.explanation: Bharati admired Tilak and belonged to the assertive nationalist current that demanded swaraj.

questions.q: Subramania Bharati's poetry promoted unity among Indians by opposing which social evil?
questions.explanation: Bharati attacked caste distinctions and untouchability, urging the equality of all Indians.

questions.q: Bharati's patriotic compositions personified India as what?
questions.explanation: Bharati famously sang in praise of Bharata Mata, or Mother India, a central image of his nationalism.

questions.q: In which year was Subramania Bharati born?
questions.explanation: Subramania Bharati was born on 11 December 1882 at Ettayapuram.

questions.q: Bharati received the title 'Bharati', meaning one blessed by the goddess of learning, from which body in his youth?
questions.explanation: The court of the Raja of Ettayapuram conferred the title 'Bharati' on him for his poetic talent as a boy.

questions.q: Bharati served as an editor of which Tamil daily newspaper, a key platform for his nationalism?
questions.explanation: Bharati worked as an editor at the Tamil daily Swadesamitran in Madras.

questions.q: To escape arrest by the British, Bharati went into exile around 1908 in which French-controlled territory?
questions.explanation: Bharati took refuge in the French enclave of Pondicherry, where he lived in exile for about a decade.

questions.q: Which fellow nationalist leader did Bharati meet at the 1906 Calcutta Congress session, deeply influencing him?
options: Sister Nivedita and Bipin Chandra Pal
questions.explanation: At the 1906 Calcutta Congress Bharati was influenced by figures of the assertive wing, and he was notably inspired by Sister Nivedita.

questions.q: Bharati edited a Tamil weekly known for its bold nationalist tone, whose name means 'India'. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Bharati edited the weekly 'India', noted for fiery nationalist content and political cartoons.

questions.q: During his Pondicherry exile, Bharati interacted with which group of revolutionaries who had also taken refuge there?
options: Bengali and other revolutionaries fleeing British arrest
questions.explanation: Pondicherry sheltered several revolutionaries fleeing British India, and Bharati associated with figures like V. V. S. Aiyar and Aurobindo.

questions.q: Bharati's long poetic works include 'Panchali Sapatham', drawn from which epic?
questions.explanation: 'Panchali Sapatham' retells the story of Draupadi's vow from the Mahabharata, used by Bharati as an allegory of the nation.

questions.q: Bharati is regarded as a pioneer of modern Tamil literature chiefly because of what stylistic contribution?
options: Using simple, vigorous language accessible to the common people
questions.explanation: Bharati transformed Tamil poetry by adopting a simple, direct style that ordinary people could understand and sing.

questions.q: Bharati wrote admiringly about freedom struggles and revolutions in other countries, including which European event?
questions.explanation: Bharati drew inspiration from world freedom movements and praised events such as the French and Russian revolutions.

questions.q: After returning from Pondicherry to British India in 1918, what initially happened to Bharati?
options: He was briefly arrested and detained
questions.explanation: On re-entering British India in 1918 Bharati was arrested and detained for a period before being released.

questions.q: Bharati's collection of devotional songs addressed to a fierce goddess is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Bharati composed 'Kali Pattu', devotional songs to the goddess Kali, alongside his Krishna-themed 'Kannan Pattu'.

questions.q: In which year did Subramania Bharati die?
questions.explanation: Subramania Bharati died on 11 September 1921 in Madras, at the age of 38.

questions.q: Bharati's death in 1921 is commonly attributed to an incident involving which animal at a temple?
questions.explanation: Bharati's health declined after he was injured by a temple elephant at Triplicane, contributing to his early death.

questions.q: Bharati's collection 'Kannan Pattu' depicts Krishna in a range of human relationships, including most strikingly as what?
options: A servant, friend, mother and beloved
questions.explanation: In 'Kannan Pattu' Bharati imagines Krishna in many intimate roles, including as servant, friend, mother and lover.

questions.q: Bharati's famous song 'Aaduvome Pallu Paaduvome' celebrates what theme?
questions.explanation: The song, meaning 'We shall dance and sing', joyously anticipates India's attainment of freedom.

questions.q: Which woman, the head of a movement Bharati met in Calcutta, did he credit with awakening his vision of an empowered Indian womanhood?
questions.explanation: Bharati dedicated work to Sister Nivedita, whom he regarded as his guru in matters of nationalism and women's dignity.

questions.q: Bharati addressed many patriotic verses to a personified figure he called by which Tamil-Sanskrit name for the motherland?
questions.explanation: Bharati extolled the Tamil land ('Senthamizh Naadu') while also venerating the broader motherland as a divine mother figure.

questions.q: The copyrights to Bharati's works were eventually handled in what notable way to spread his writings?
options: They were nationalized by the government and made freely available
questions.explanation: The Madras government nationalized the copyright of Bharati's works in the late 1940s, freeing them for wide publication.

questions.q: Bharati's prose writings notably included early Tamil examples of which literary form?
questions.explanation: Bharati is regarded as a pioneer of the modern Tamil short story and essay, in addition to his poetry.
